author: @stevetobias4890
text:
I am wondering if the coils will be wired in series or parallel. Will need to make sure that the wiring is all traveling in the same direction so that no coils cancel each other out. I think that the output will be really good. Possibly better than a solar panel.
Thanks yet again Rob, great video.
Edit ** you have to take into account that the 30 ma is the rotor moving at a snails pace. Put it into a decent breeze or a mild to strong wind and the output of the one coil would increase dramatically. Also when hooking the coils together with the magnets being in the good old N, S, N, D configuration it should put out a  wave form that is close to a pure sinusoidal wave which is ready for DC rectification using the alernetating power directly wouldn't be good because the frequency wouldn't be consistent and could potentially damaging.

replies.author: @MrTubeuser12
replies.text: I was thinking the same, they could be wired - all series, all parallel or half and half, depends on what voltage/current ratio you want, which I guess would depend on it's application. will definitely need rectification on the output.

replies.author: @stevetobias4890
replies.text:
@MrTubeuser12 agree, but you have to admit that this is an awesome project for generating decent power. I also agree with coils being wired in series or every second in banks of parallel with them put in series.
Another possibility is adding larger blades (about 1-3 cm) to every second to third vent would increase the area for the wind to increase the potential to increase the speed of the*turbine* (yes I use the term loosely). Decent bearings would be needed to reduce friction also.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I should have mentioned I paid particular attention to the way the coils were wound and mounted so I know the coil winding direction - but I will mention in the next vid - good point mate

author: @bernardstokes6550
text: Two thoughts come to mind and not wishing to detract from your considerable talent here.....heat and soot ....I know that my rotating cowl gets some soot buildup over a season and I am really not sure how hot the final exhaust temperatures will be but at 2.5 meters from the top it can be well over 100 degrees after a good days burning....wood not coal.

replies.author: @bernardstokes6550
replies.text: Desmond Bagley yes I see that now you say it...I had not thought of that because I was caught up with my own cowl on my chimney, probably wise to externalise the design if being used on an active chimney.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I wouldn't use this on a chimney with a fire mate - as you say too much soot and heat - it would certainly be a good idea to mount the rings external to the cowl if you are using this on an active chimney - but good point - cheers

author: @thornhedge9639
text: Nice build Robert.   Kinda concerned about the drag potential of the extra fan unit you're placing in the throat of this whirly bird though; is it veined properly to run the same direction as the outer rotor? It may  interfere anyway as the whole thing works on heat rise rather than wind (although wind doesn't hurt obviously).  I am rooting for you though!!!!!

replies.author: @stevetobias4890
replies.text: Good insight, maybe if you need to have fan blades it would be better to have 2 or 3 sets of thinner twin blade propellers as used in RC drones set at 90 degrees.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: the whole thing is a prototype mate - I am 100% certain there is a lot wrong with it - but it's how I learn by doing - I have already learn a fair bit myself and to be honest I am not sure if the fan is right or not - but I have that one and that's the one I am going to use - basically we will see and if it doesn't work out we can alway remove it

replies.author: @thornhedge9639
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering   I love the whole concept Robert.   The fact that you actually went the extra distance to build it at all is more than most of us ever get around to; so my admiration as always abounds my friend.  My thought was only whether or not the fans were layed out to work together or whether their alignment (blade pitch) would be counter to each other.  Ya?  Since they are not independent having them pitched against each other would be counter productive and I can't tell from the video whether they are aligned to work together or against each other..   So it was a poorly worded question my friend and not a criticism.  Love your work!
